course_info.title: Physics
course_info.description: The ZIMSEC Advanced Level Physics syllabus emphasizes the mastery and application of physics principles, fostering analytical and problem-solving skills for further studies in Science and Technology. It aims to develop creative and innovative learners who can apply physics concepts in industrial processes for value addition and economic growth.

topics.title: Physical Quantities and Units
topics.description: Learners will express derived units, check equation homogeneity, use standard prefixes, and perform vector operations.

topics.title: Errors and Uncertainties
topics.description: Learners will distinguish between systematic and random errors, differentiate precision and accuracy, and assess uncertainties in derived quantities.

topics.title: Kinematics
topics.description: Learners will derive and apply equations for uniformly accelerated motion in a straight line, including free fall and projectile motion.

topics.title: Dynamics
topics.description: Learners will define linear momentum and impulse, apply Newton's laws of motion, and use the principle of conservation of momentum in collisions.

topics.title: Forces
topics.description: Learners will describe various types of forces, understand equilibrium, apply Archimedes' Principle, explain viscosity and terminal velocity, and calculate moments and torques.

topics.title: Work, Energy and Power
topics.description: Learners will define and calculate work, derive and apply formulas for kinetic and potential energy, explain internal energy, and relate power to work done and efficiency.

topics.title: Circular Motion
topics.description: Learners will define angular motion concepts, derive and apply centripetal force and acceleration equations, and describe everyday examples of circular motion.

topics.title: Gravitational Field
topics.description: Learners will understand gravitational fields, apply Newton's law of gravitation, analyze circular orbits, and define gravitational field strength and potential.

topics.title: Oscillations
topics.description: Learners will describe simple harmonic motion, define related terms like amplitude and frequency, understand damped and forced oscillations, and explain resonance.

topics.title: Waves
topics.description: Learners will understand wave properties, critical angle and total internal reflection, polarisation, the electromagnetic spectrum, and X-ray production and applications.

topics.title: Superposition
topics.description: Learners will explain wave superposition, stationary waves, two-source interference (Young's experiment), coherence, diffraction, and principles of CT scanning.

topics.title: Electricity
topics.description: Learners will describe electrostatic phenomena, define charge, potential difference, resistance, EMF, power, and internal resistance, and solve related circuit problems.

topics.title: D.C. Circuits
topics.description: Learners will apply Kirchhoff's laws to solve DC circuit problems, understand potential dividers, and utilize potentiometers for comparing potential differences.

topics.title: Electric Fields
topics.description: Learners will describe electric fields and field strength, apply Coulomb's law, and understand electric potential and potential gradient.

topics.title: Capacitances
topics.description: Learners will understand capacitance, function of capacitors in series and parallel circuits, energy storage in capacitors, and their charging and discharging behavior.
